steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The subject of the sentence is 'Olympia Brown of ______.' The blank must complete the state name (Connecticut) and then connect the subject directly to its verb. The question tests whether any punctuation should separate the subject from the verb.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Connecticut. Played": a period creates a full stop, leaving 'played a role...' without a subject — a sentence fragment. ✗
  • "Connecticut played": no punctuation between subject and verb — correct. One complete independent clause results. ✓
  • "Connecticut; played": a semicolon separates independent clauses, not a subject from its verb — grammatically incorrect here. ✗
  • "Connecticut: played": a colon introduces lists or explanations after an independent clause — it cannot separate a subject from its verb. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
